name: Hawaii Loa Ridge
desc: Gated ridgeline community — panoramic views, privacy, security, seriously wealthy enclave above Hawaii Kai.
insider: The guard gate operates 24/7 and you must be on a resident's guest list to enter — there is no talking your way in, so coordinate ahead of time
Sunrise from the upper ridge lots puts you above the morning cloud layer with views stretching from Diamond Head to Molokai on clear days
Wild pigs roam the upper slopes and can destroy landscaping overnight — most residents fence their gardens and secure trash bins
The community pool, tennis courts, and small clubhouse are well-maintained and almost never crowded since only about 300 homes share them
Homes here start well above $2M and go up to $8M+ — the HOA fees are substantial but cover security, road maintenance, and common areas
The single access road means if there's an accident or maintenance, you're stuck until it clears — plan accordingly
The elevation (400-800 feet) brings noticeably cooler temperatures and stronger trade winds than Hawaii Kai below — you'll want a light jacket in winter evenings
Several tech executives and retired professionals from the mainland have settled here for the privacy — it's the kind of quiet where you can hear birdsong
Groceries and dining require driving down to Hawaii Kai's Koko Marina or Costco — there are zero commercial services on the ridge itself
The Norfolk pine and ironwood trees lining the roads make the drive up feel like entering a different world from the suburban sprawl below
